seo.fallbackTitle: Torah Roll Helper | Assistant de roulement de la Torah | עוזר גלילת התורה
seo.fallbackDescription: Free Torah scroll rolling assistant for Gabbaim and Ba'alei Kriah. Calculate the direction and number of columns to roll between Torah readings. Also known as Assistant de roulement de la Torah and עוזר גלילת התורה.
seo.keywords: Torah roll helper,Torah roll assistant,assistant de roulement de la Torah,עוזר גלילת התורה,Torah rolling assistant,Sefer Torah,Gabbai,Ba'al Koreh,גלילת התורה

home.title: Torah Roll Helper | Free Sefer Torah rolling assistant
home.description: Free tool for Gabbaim and Ba'alei Kriah to calculate how many Torah columns to roll and in which direction between readings.

about.title: About | Torah Roll Helper
about.description: Learn what Torah Roll Helper does, where its Torah reading data comes from, and how to contact or contribute to the project.

howTo.title: How To Use | Torah Roll Helper
howTo.description: Learn how to use Torah Roll Helper to identify the current Torah position, choose the next reading, and get clear rolling instructions.

about.title: About
about.description: The Torah Rolling Assistant is designed for Gabbaim and Ba'alei Kriah to reduce Tircha d'Tzibbur. Given the current Torah position and the next reading, it calculates the exact rolling direction and number of columns required.
about.dataCreditsPrefix: The database for this project is based on open-source files from
about.dataCreditsMiddle: , which in turn utilize data from
about.dataCreditsThanks: Our thanks go to the tikkun.io project for sharing their work with the community.
about.issueInvite: If you have an improvement idea or wish to add a language, please open an issue on GitHub.
about.emailInvite: For direct contact, you are welcome to reach us by email.
about.dictaThanksSuffix: website for making their API available and for inspiring this project.

dicta.title: Dicta Illuminate
dicta.noResultSubtitle: Try again following the instructions, or take a picture of a different part of the page.
dicta.idleSubtitle: Use the frame lines to align text before capture.
dicta.cameraUnavailable: Camera API is not available in this browser context.
dicta.cameraHttpsHint: Camera authorization is not available on localhost. Open the app over HTTPS instead of localhost.
dicta.cameraPermissionHint: Your device might be blocking camera access before the browser can even ask. Open your device settings, find your browser app, and allow Camera access. If you already blocked this site inside the browser, re-enable it in the browser's site settings.

instructions.1: Fit the line width inside the white frame.
instructions.2: Align the text using the yellow lines.
instructions.3: Capture at least four lines of text.
instructions.4: Ensure the first line is full (no large blanks).

dicta.chooseSubtitle: Multiple matches were found. Select the correct page.

howTo.title: How To Use
howTo.subtitle: Follow these quick steps to get clear rolling instructions.

steps.step1: Identify the current location of the Torah scroll (the parasha it is currently open on) using the camera, exact manual input, or the page's first words.
steps.step2: Select your target reading from the upcoming list or the full index.
steps.step3: The app calculates the rolling direction (Forward/Backward) and the number of columns to roll.

howTo.tip: Tip: update FROM first, then choose TO.

camera.title: Camera usage
camera.step1: Allow camera access when prompted by your browser.
camera.step2: Place the Torah text inside the frame in good, even lighting.
camera.step3: Hold steady for a moment so detection can lock correctly.
camera.step4: If detection fails, use the page's first words or manual input to set the current location.

tutorials.title: Interactive screen tours
tutorials.subtitle: These tours guide you through the actual app interface. Use the short tour for the basic workflow, or the full tour to master every tool.

tutorialPrompt.title: New here?
tutorialPrompt.text: Guided tours are available in the 'How To' page. You can also start a quick tour right now to learn the basics.

step1.title: Language selection
step1.description: Click or tap here to change the app's language. The tour will pause so you can open the menu.

step2.title: Choose your language
step2.description: Select your preferred language. The tutorial text will update immediately. If current language is good, tap Next.

step3.title: Set the current scroll location
step3.description: This is the FROM side. Use 'Choose manually' for all readings, 'Camera' to identify the open column from the Klaf, or 'Input' for an exact reference, page number, or the page's first words. The cards below show last month's readings for quick access.

step4.title: Choose your destination
step4.description: This is the TO side. This calendar strip shows upcoming readings. Most of the time, you will simply click the next Parasha here. We've selected an example for you.

step5.title: Your rolling instructions
step5.description: This card provides your result: the number of Amudim (columns) to move, the rolling direction, and the exact FROM and TO pages used for the calculation.

step1.title: Language selection
step1.description: Click or tap here to change the app's language. The tour will pause so you can open the menu.

step2.title: Choose your language
step2.description: Select your preferred language. The tutorial text will update immediately. If current language is good, tap Next.

step3.title: The FROM card
step3.description: Use this section to define where the Sefer Torah is currently open. The top buttons offer three ways to find a reading, while the cards below show last month's readings for a quick selection.

step4.title: The full reading list
step4.description: Click 'Choose manually' when you need to find a specific Parasha, Yom Tov, Rosh Chodesh, fast day, or special Maftir.

step5.title: The index overlay
step5.description: Use the search bar at the top, or expand the categories below to find the exact Kriah you need.

step6.title: Page recognition (Camera)
step6.description: Click the camera icon when the Sefer Torah is open and you want the app to identify your location directly from the text on the Klaf.

step7.title: Camera results
step7.description: After taking a photo, the app scans the text and suggests the most likely Amudim for your current location. If the photo misses, the same screen now offers a direct fallback to typing the page's first words.

step8.title: Manual input
step8.description: Click 'Input' when you already know the exact Perek/Pasuk, the specific page number in the Tikkun Korim, or when you want to search from the line that opens the page.

step9.title: Exact location dialog
step9.description: Here you can set the Sefer, Perek, and Pasuk, jump straight to a page number, or switch to searching from the line that opens the page.

step10.title: Search by first words
step10.description: Start typing the first words that appear at the top of the page. Each result shows the line that opens that page. If needed, open the built-in Hebrew keyboard or switch to match anywhere inside that line.

step11.title: The TO card
step11.description: This is where you set your destination. The calendar strip displays the upcoming month. In most cases, you will simply select the next reading from here.

step12.title: Reference point
step12.description: Choose your exact stopping point. You can target the start of the Parasha, the end of a weekday reading (Sheni/Hamishi/Mincha), or the end of the full Shabbat reading.

step13.title: The result card
step13.description: Once FROM and TO are set, this card gives you the final answer: how many Amudim to roll, which direction, and the reference pages.

step14.title: Crossing into a new Chumash
step14.description: If your roll crosses into a new book (e.g., from Exodus to Leviticus), this note tells you how many pages are left to roll after reaching target book.

step15.title: Page preview
step15.description: Click the page number or the small book icon to see a preview of the selected column.

step16.title: Verify with the Tikkun
step16.description: The preview shows the first line of the column (with an option to toggle Nikud) and includes a button to open a full digital Tikkun layout to verify your spot.

step17.title: Settings
step17.description: Click the cog icon to open your preferences.

step18.title: Community customs
step18.description: Use settings to toggle between Israel and Diaspora (Gola) modes, select your Nusach, and choose the scroll layout (e.g., 245 columns). These options affect which readings appear and how the roll is calculated.

step19.title: Navigation
step19.description: Use the navigation bar to access other tools. On mobile, tap the menu icon to open the sidebar.

step20.title: About & Contact
step20.description: When you finish this tour, the 'About' page will open so you can see project details, view data credits, or report an issue on GitHub.

help.gola: Enable this if you are outside Israel to synchronize the weekly Parasha readings and special Diaspora holiday readings with the Diaspora calendar.
help.nusach: Choose your reading tradition. Right now, only Sefaradic is available.
help.torahType: Most sifrei Torah use the רמ"ה layout (245 pages). Other layouts, like רמ"ח (248), can be added later.

result.title: Rolling Instructions
result.noData: Select both FROM and TO to compute roll

preview.lineUnavailable: First line is unavailable for this page.
preview.shiftTip: Tip: Hold Shift to toggle quickly

manual.title: Enter Location Manually
manual.page_not_found: Page not found for the selected reference.

firstLineSearch.title: Search by First Words
firstLineSearch.description: Type the first words that appear at the top of the page. Each result shows the line that opens that page.
firstLineSearch.emptyState: Find the page from its opening words
firstLineSearch.emptyStateHint: Type the beginning of the line that starts the page.
firstLineSearch.keepTyping: Type a bit more to narrow the results.
firstLineSearch.noMatchesHint: Try fewer words, or switch to searching anywhere in the opening line.
firstLineSearch.searchAnywhere: Search anywhere in the opening line
